channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Falcons name rookie Michael Penix Jr. as starting QB, benching veteran Kirk Cousins
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — The Atlanta Falcons are turning to rookie Michael Penix Jr. as their starting quarterback and benching veteran Kirk Cousins as they fight for their first playoff berth since 2017. Coach Raheem Morris announced the decision in a statement Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES ODUM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: MLB study: Velocity, max efforts likely causing pitching injuries; rule changes should be considered
item.description: A yearlong study by Major League Baseball has concluded rising velocities, pitch shaping and emphasis on maximum effort are the likely causes of the vast increase in pitcher injuries and recommended exploring rules changes to address the problem.
item.dc:creator: Ronald Blum, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US defender Becky Sauerbrunn retires from professional soccer after a 16-year international career
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Becky Sauerbrunn, a former captain of the U.S. women's team who played in three Olympics and three Women’s World Cups, announced her retirement Tuesday from professional soccer after a 16-year international career.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NBA announces All-Star tournament plan for this season: 4 teams, 3 games, 1 night
item.description: The NBA’s All-Star Game is going to be an All-Star tournament this season, with the league announcing that it has finalized plans to use a different format for the upcoming midseason showcase in San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: Tim Reynolds, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rafael Nadal says he thought about taking a mental health break a few years ago
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Rafael Nadal thought about taking a mental health break from tennis a few years ago but “conquered it by always moving forward” and “slowly became myself again,” the 22-time Grand Slam champion writes in an essay posted online Tuesday, less than a month after the last match of his ca
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Isaac Wilson decides to return to Utah
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Upon second thought, Isaac Wilson is returning to Utah. Eight days after announcing he was entering the transfer portal, Wilson announced that he is returning to Utah, where will compete for the starting quarterback job with New Mexico transfer Devon Dampier.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Saquon Barkley slowed by 'minor' injury, loses ground in pursuit of Dickerson's record
item.description: Eagles running back Saquon Barkley had a setback in his chase of the NFL season rushing record when he appeared slowed by an undisclosed injury.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Vintage performances by Davante Adams and Aaron Rodgers carry Jets past defenseless Jaguars 32-25
item.description: Davante Adams and Aaron Rodgers gave the New York Jets' next general manager and head coach something to ponder with a glimpse into the past — and potentially the future.
item.dc:creator: MARK LONG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: BYU gets defensive against Wyoming in 68-49 win
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Mawot Mag and Dawson Baker came off the bench and combined for 22 points, sparking BYU men’s basketball to 68-49 win over Wyoming on Saturday night at the Delta Center. "It was a good win," said BYU head coach Kevin Young.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Dick Vitale says he's cancer-free after 4 bouts with disease in just over 3 years
item.description: Vitale had surgery in the summer to remove cancerous lymph nodes from his neck. He was previously treated for melanoma and lymphoma, and had radiation treatments last year for vocal cord cancer.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 1 school board gives necessary approval on Arlington Park property tax deal with Bears; 2 more to go
item.description: PALATINE, Ill. — The Bears have moved one step closer to a property tax agreement on the 326-acre site of the former Arlington International Racecourse in northwest suburban Arlington Heights.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Wander Franco trial postponed due to no-show witnesses
item.description: Tampa Bay Rays shortstop Wander Franco is scheduled to go to trial Thursday in the Dominican Republic in a sexual abuse case involving a 14-year-old girl.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New Mexico QB Devon Dampier transferring to Utah
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Utes football team has found its new starting quarterback. Devon Dampier announced that he is transferring from New Mexico to Utah, and will have two years of eligibility remaining.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
